We have a family with children in our district who lost their home to a fire last night and they lost everything. They are in need of clothes and other essentials. If you want to donate to the family, you can drop items off at the district office any time today or during the parade tonight. Here are the sizes of clothing that they would need:

Male: Mens shirt size XXL and pant size 40
Female: Women's shirt size 2XXL/size 18/20, shoe size 11, and pant size 18/20
Girl teenager: Shirt size women's Large, shoe size 9, pant size 14
Boy youth: Shirt size youth Large/ size 18, shoe size youth 8, pant size 18 youth
Infant girl: 24 months

This situation is continuously changing. Every day brings new information and new considerations for how to best approach educating our students. We know the uncertainty is difficult and we are working hard to share information as we are able to. Thank you for your patience and support as we work together to offer online instruction and feed our students while doing our best to keep our amazing staff, students and families safe and healthy.

For #NationalSchoolChoiceWeek, we're highlighting our Early College High School Program, which offers students the chance to graduate simultaneously with a high school diploma and an associate degree—all for free! We partner with Austin Community College to operate seven AISD ECHS campuses: Akins, Crockett, Eastside Memorial, Navarro, LBJ, Travis and Northeast.

¡Nuestro programa de Escuela Preparatoria de Universidad Temprana (ECHS, por sus siglas en inglés) les ofrece a los estudiantes la oportunidad de graduarse simultáneamente con un diploma de preparatoria y con un título técnico superior, completamente gratis! Trabajamos en colaboración con el Austin Community College para operar siete planteles con ECHS del AISD: Akins, Crockett, Eastside Memorial, Navarro, LBJ, Travis y Northeast.

Learn more / Aprende más: www.austinisd.org/school-choice/echs #ChooseAISD #AISDProud #AISDGameChangers

Parents, students, staff and community members who are interested in signing up to speak before the Board of Education at either public meeting must email boardofeducation@rcsdk12.org by noon the day of the meeting to participate.

This meeting will be held via Zoom conferencing.

Topic: The Fiscal Status of RCSD

Parents, students, staff and community members who are interested in signing up to speak at the public hearing must e-mail statemonitor@rcsdk12.org no later than 12:00 p.m. noon the day of the hearing. More information is available by visiting www.rcsdk12.org/statemonitorhearings.

The public may submit public comment or testimony prior to the hearing via email to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org or via regular mail sent to:

Attn: State Monitor Public Hearing
131 West Broad Street
Rochester, NY 14614

Questions regarding the hearings may also be directed to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org.

During the Zoom hearing, persons who are providing public comment will be given three minutes to speak. If you need an accommodation to participate and/or view the public hearing, send your accommodation request to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org or call 585-262-8525.

This meeting will be held via Zoom conferencing.

Topic: The Academic Status of RCSD

Parents, students, staff and community members who are interested in signing up to speak at the public hearing must e-mail statemonitor@rcsdk12.org no later than 12:00 p.m. noon the day of the hearing. More information is available by visiting www.rcsdk12.org/statemonitorhearings.

The public may submit public comment or testimony prior to the hearing via email to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org or via regular mail sent to:

Attn: State Monitor Public Hearing
131 West Broad Street
Rochester, NY 14614

Questions regarding the hearings may also be directed to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org.

During the Zoom hearing, persons who are providing public comment will be given three minutes to speak. If you need an accommodation to participate and/or view the public hearing, send your accommodation request to statemonitor@rcsdk12.org or call 585-262-8525.

This meeting will be held via Zoom conferencing.

As a result of the new statewide limitations put in effect by Governor Wolf's office, we have made the difficult decision to cancel the prom. The committee and administration have been working hard to make this a reality, but unfortunately, it is no longer feasible to host this event. Any student who purchased a ticket will receive a full refund. Any questions, please contact Mrs. Brazell, Mr. Blakley, or Ms. Simons. The new mandates only allow for groups of 25 people indoors. We have been working hard since March to make this important event happen, but at this point, we are at the end of the road. We are all so very sorry.
Refunds will be issued within the next two weeks.
--

The Wayne County Board of Education Special Called Meeting (May 22, 2020) is live. The meeting is also being broadcast live on Channel 18 on Spectrum Cable.

Special School Board Meeting
ORDER OF BUSINESS
1. Call to order
2. Pledge
3. Discussion regarding District 8 long term plan.
4. Public Comment.
5. Appoint Signatory in absence of Superintendent.
6. Approve Mr. Goetz as administrative consultant.
7. Discussion regarding KrisAnn Norby-Jahner as District 8 legal counsel on all matters concerning reorganization.
8. Approve pursuit of reorganization with Williston Public School District #1.
9. Request proposal from Paul Stremick to act as reorganization consultant/mediator on behalf of District 8 during development of reorganization plan.
10. Approve process of discussing land transfers with neighboring school districts.
11. Request meeting with Jodi Johnson, Jeff Lind, and District 1 School Board to discuss reorganization process/plan.
12. Approve bi-weekly (every other week) meetings with District 1 School Board.
13. Discussion regarding Reorganization Task Force.
14. Adjourn.

ORDER OF BUSINESS

1. Call to order
2. Pledge
3. Explanation of Public Comment (via chat feature)
4. Availability of D8 funds for D1 HS expansion in conjunction with a signed JPA
5. Transportation routes, number of students, operation cost, funding aid, etc.
6. Issues for next meeting
7. Adjourn

Join Zoom Meeting

Meeting ID: 974 1914 2262

ORDER OF BUSINESS

1. Call to order
2. Pledge
3. Work Session with Paul Stremick
a. Review of state Aid Worksheets
b. Comparative Study Assumptions
i. Enrollment
ii. County Revenue
iii. Taxable Valuation
iv. Mill Levies
v. Bus Stops/Students by other Districts in District #8

vii. Taxable Valuation by Township
viii. Possible Dissolution/Land Transfer Valuation Changes
ix. Taxable Valuation/Mill Levies for School Districts in Williams County
x. Baseline Data
xi. Other
4. Adjourn

ORDER OF BUSINESS

1. Call to order
2. Pledge
3. Joint employment of Superintendent for District 1 and District 8 discussion
4. County commission decision to postpone joint funding project
5. WHS enrollment of outside district residents for 2020-2021 school year
6. Adjourn

Today is Wild n' Out Wednesday! How many jelly beans do you think are in the jar? Post your guess!

Join us at 7pm for a Family Game Night of Trivia for Kids.

Trivia questions are aimed towards kids.

Example of how to answer in the comments sections.
Let say the correct answer is number 1 "Arm".

Here are acceptable options of comment:
"1 Arm"
"ARM"
"arm"
"1"
"arm i guess"
"i'm sure it is aRm"

Not acceptable options of comment:
"ArmIThink"
"1arm"
"Armmmmm"
"111111"

As you see we ignore letter case and can use just a number or a just text to identify the answer.
